core_mechanism: randomized-truncation (Russian-roulette) debiasing of a convergent sequence: the limit of a biased approximating sequence is expressed as a telescoping sum of increments, and an unbiased estimator of the limit is formed by drawing a random truncation index and reweighting the retained increments by the reciprocal of that index's tail probability
brief_reason: The abstract takes a process (numerical scheme, Monte-Carlo integral, or iterative root-finding) that converges to a limiting value and produces an unbiased estimator of that limit with standard errors, i.e. it removes the bias of a truncated approximation to a limit rather than of any specific quantity.
